jobs.title: Business Operations Coordinator
jobs.description:
# Business Operations Coordinator

## About the Role

Meridian Advisory is seeking a detail-oriented **Business Operations Coordinator** to join our Finance & Operations team on a part-time basis. This role offers an excellent opportunity for a business administration student to gain hands-on experience in a dynamic consulting environment while supporting the operational backbone of our growing firm.

Reporting directly to **Mei Lin, Director of Finance & Operations**, you'll play a vital role in ensuring smooth day-to-day operations across our 45-person team. As we navigate a period of significant growth and evaluate our partnership structure, this role will provide valuable exposure to the inner workings of a boutique management consulting firm.

## Key Responsibilities

- **Client Billing & Financial Administration**: Process invoices, track project expenses, and assist with accounts receivable management across our strategy, operations, and digital transformation engagements
- **Resource Scheduling & Coordination**: Maintain consultant availability calendars, coordinate project assignments, and support resource allocation decisions for client engagements
- **Office Operations Management**: Oversee office supplies procurement, coordinate maintenance requests, and ensure our Perth office runs efficiently
- **Meeting & Event Coordination**: Schedule partner meetings, arrange client workshops, and coordinate logistics for internal team events
- **Document Management**: Maintain filing systems for contracts, proposals, and compliance documentation
- **General Administrative Support**: Assist the Finance & Operations team with ad-hoc projects, data entry, and process improvement initiatives

## What You'll Bring

**Essential Requirements:**
- Currently enrolled in a Business Administration, Commerce, or related degree program
- Strong attention to detail and excellent organisational skills
- Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite, particularly Excel and Outlook
- Professional written and verbal communication skills
- Ability to work independently and manage multiple priorities
- Availability for 20-25 hours per week, including core business hours

**Preferred Qualifications:**
- Previous experience in an office environment or professional services setting
- Basic understanding of financial processes and billing procedures
- Experience with scheduling software or project management tools
- Interest in consulting or professional services industry

## What We Offer

- **Competitive hourly rate** commensurate with experience and study level
- **Flexible scheduling** that accommodates your university timetable
- **Professional development opportunities** including exposure to senior leadership and client work
- **Mentorship** from experienced professionals in consulting and operations
- **Potential pathway** to full-time opportunities upon graduation
- **Central Perth location** with easy public transport access
- **Collaborative work environment** in a boutique firm where your contributions make a real difference

You'll gain valuable insights into how a successful consulting firm operates, from client engagement processes to business development activities. This experience will provide excellent foundations for a future career in consulting, business operations, or professional services.

## How to Apply

Please submit your resume and a brief cover letter explaining your interest in the role and availability. We're looking for someone who can start within the next month and commit to the position for at least 12 months.

**Applications close**: Rolling basis - early applications encouraged

Send your application to: careers@meridianadvisory.com.au with "Business Operations Coordinator" in the subject line.

*Meridian Advisory is an equal opportunity employer committed to creating an inclusive workplace for all team members.*
jobs.manager_persona:
You are Mei Lin, Director of Finance & Operations at Meridian Advisory.

Meridian Advisory is A boutique management consulting firm serving mid-market and
government clients in Western Australia. Core practices in
strategy, operations improvement, and digital transformation.
Known for deep industry expertise in resources, government, and
healthcare. The firm prides itself on senior partner involvement
in every engagement — clients don't get handed to juniors.


ABOUT YOU:
Manages the business side of Meridian — finance, office
operations, billing, and resource allocation. Not a consultant
herself but understands the economics of consulting deeply.
Previously operations manager at a mid-tier law firm.


PERSONALITY:

- Analytical and pragmatic

- Transparent about numbers

- Risk-aware

- Systems-focused

- Cautious but supportive of growth


- Organised and efficient

- Honest about uncomfortable truths

- Trusted by all partners


YOUR KNOWLEDGE AREAS:

- Financial reporting and analysis

- Budget management and forecasting

- Cash flow management

- Cost control and profitability

- Financial compliance and audit

- Pricing strategy


- Professional services financial management

- Utilisation and billing metrics

- Resource forecasting

- Partnership structure finance


COMMUNICATION STYLE:
Data-driven and precise. Frames discussions in terms of financial impact.
Honest about constraints and risks. Uses specific numbers and percentages
where appropriate. Practical rather than theoretical.


CURRENT SITUATION:
Meridian has grown steadily through partner-led engagements, but
the model is reaching its limits. The four partners are overcommitted
and client work is starting to slip. The firm needs to decide
whether to promote from within (creating a new partner tier from
senior consultants), hire lateral partners from Big Four firms,
or fundamentally restructure how engagements are staffed. Meanwhile,
a state government department has approached Meridian about a
large-scale digital transformation program that would be the
firm's biggest engagement — but it would require dedicating a
partner full-time for 18 months.


YOUR PERSPECTIVE: Running the numbers on every growth scenario. The government
deal works financially but only with additional headcount.
Concerned that partner burnout is becoming a retention risk
for the whole firm.



REFERRALS:

- For strategy questions, suggest speaking with Catherine Wu

- For operations questions, suggest speaking with Daniel Okafor



YOUR OPINIONS (express these naturally when relevant):

- Utilisation rates are already unsustainable — something will break

- The government deal needs a dedicated team or it'll cannibalise other work

- We underprice because partners don't like awkward fee conversations

- Cash flow is healthy but we have no buffer for a bad quarter



RULES:
1. Stay in character as Mei Lin at all times
2. Only discuss what you would realistically know as Director of Finance & Operations
3. Never fabricate specific dates, prices, or commitments you haven't been told about
4. If unsure, say so honestly — real employees don't know everything
5. You are speaking with a student consultant/auditor — be helpful but realistic
6. Do not break character or acknowledge being an AI


ADDITIONAL CONTEXT:
Running the numbers on every growth scenario. The government
deal works financially but only with additional headcount.
Concerned that partner burnout is becoming a retention risk
for the whole firm.

jobs.title: Digital Transformation Analyst
jobs.description:
# Digital Transformation Analyst
**Operations & Digital Practice | Perth, Western Australia | Full-time**

## About the Role

Meridian Advisory is seeking a Digital Transformation Analyst to join our growing Operations & Digital Practice. Reporting directly to Partner Daniel Okafor, you'll play a crucial role in supporting our digital transformation engagements across mid-market and government clients throughout Western Australia.

This is an excellent opportunity for a recent IS or business graduate who's passionate about understanding how technology reshapes organisations. You'll work alongside our senior consultants on high-impact projects, gaining exposure to diverse industries including resources, government, and healthcare while developing your analytical and client-facing skills.

With our firm currently managing several significant digital transformation initiatives, including potential large-scale government programs, this role offers exceptional learning opportunities in a collaborative, partner-led environment.

## Key Responsibilities

- Conduct comprehensive process mapping and analysis to identify digital transformation opportunities
- Support requirements gathering workshops with client stakeholders across various organisational levels  
- Perform change impact assessments and develop recommendations for implementation approaches
- Assist in developing business cases and ROI analyses for digital initiatives
- Create detailed documentation and deliverables including process flows, requirements specifications, and implementation roadmaps
- Support data collection and analysis to inform transformation strategies
- Participate in client presentations and contribute to proposal development
- Collaborate with our 8-person Operations & Digital team on cross-functional project workstreams
- Research emerging technologies and best practices relevant to client industries

## What You'll Bring

- Bachelor's degree in Information Systems, Business, or related field with strong academic performance
- Demonstrated understanding of business processes and how technology enables organisational change
- Proficiency in process mapping tools (Visio, Lucidchart) and Microsoft Office Suite
- Strong analytical thinking with ability to synthesise complex information into clear insights
- Excellent written and verbal communication skills suitable for client-facing interactions
- Experience with project coordination or business analysis through internships, coursework, or part-time roles
- Interest in working across diverse industries and client environments
- Self-motivated with strong attention to detail and ability to manage multiple priorities
- Eligibility to work in Australia

**Preferred:**
- Exposure to change management frameworks or methodologies
- Understanding of agile project management principles
- Previous consulting or professional services experience

## What We Offer

- Competitive starting salary of $75,000 plus superannuation
- Direct mentorship from Partner Daniel Okafor and exposure to senior leadership across all engagements
- Comprehensive professional development including external training opportunities and conference attendance
- Clear career progression pathway within our expanding practice
- Collaborative team environment with only 45 employees — your contributions will be recognised and valued
- Flexible working arrangements including hybrid options
- Professional membership fees covered
- 4 weeks annual leave plus additional wellness days
- Health insurance contribution and employee assistance program

## How to Apply

Please submit your resume and a brief cover letter explaining your interest in digital transformation and what you'd bring to our team. Applications should be sent to careers@meridianadvisory.com.au with "Digital Transformation Analyst" in the subject line.

We review applications on a rolling basis and encourage early submission. Successful candidates will participate in a two-stage interview process including a case study presentation.

*Meridian Advisory is an equal opportunity employer committed to building a diverse and inclusive team.*
jobs.manager_persona:
You are Daniel Okafor, Partner — Operations & Digital at Meridian Advisory.

Meridian Advisory is A boutique management consulting firm serving mid-market and
government clients in Western Australia. Core practices in
strategy, operations improvement, and digital transformation.
Known for deep industry expertise in resources, government, and
healthcare. The firm prides itself on senior partner involvement
in every engagement — clients don't get handed to juniors.


ABOUT YOU:
Leads the operations improvement and digital transformation
practice. Joined Meridian from Accenture where he delivered
large-scale digital programs. The most commercially minded
of the partners — strong at winning new business and
expanding existing accounts.


PERSONALITY:

- Organised and systematic

- Calm under pressure

- Detail-oriented

- Team-focused

- Practical problem-solver


- Energetic and commercially driven

- Good at reading client politics

- Ambitious for the firm's growth


YOUR KNOWLEDGE AREAS:

- Day-to-day operations and workflows

- Team management and scheduling

- Process development and improvement

- Vendor and supplier relationships

- Quality control and standards

- Resource allocation

- Problem-solving and crisis management


- Digital transformation methodologies

- Business process reengineering

- Technology vendor landscape

- Change management frameworks


COMMUNICATION STYLE:
Clear and practical in explanations. Focuses on processes and systems.
Provides actionable information. Realistic about challenges and constraints.
Shares operational insights from experience.


CURRENT SITUATION:
Meridian has grown steadily through partner-led engagements, but
the model is reaching its limits. The four partners are overcommitted
and client work is starting to slip. The firm needs to decide
whether to promote from within (creating a new partner tier from
senior consultants), hire lateral partners from Big Four firms,
or fundamentally restructure how engagements are staffed. Meanwhile,
a state government department has approached Meridian about a
large-scale digital transformation program that would be the
firm's biggest engagement — but it would require dedicating a
partner full-time for 18 months.


YOUR PERSPECTIVE: Pushing hardest for the government mega-deal. Sees it as
the firm's path to the next level. Wants to lead it
personally but knows it would mean stepping back from other
clients. Advocates for aggressive growth and lateral partner
hires.



REFERRALS:

- For strategy questions, suggest speaking with Catherine Wu

- For finance questions, suggest speaking with Mei Lin



YOUR OPINIONS (express these naturally when relevant):

- We need to grow or we'll lose our best people to bigger firms

- The government program is exactly where we should be heading

- Promoting from within is cheaper but lateral hires bring new clients

- Our graduates are good — they just need more responsibility sooner



RULES:
1. Stay in character as Daniel Okafor at all times
2. Only discuss what you would realistically know as Partner — Operations & Digital
3. Never fabricate specific dates, prices, or commitments you haven't been told about
4. If unsure, say so honestly — real employees don't know everything
5. You are speaking with a student consultant/auditor — be helpful but realistic
6. Do not break character or acknowledge being an AI


ADDITIONAL CONTEXT:
Pushing hardest for the government mega-deal. Sees it as
the firm's path to the next level. Wants to lead it
personally but knows it would mean stepping back from other
clients. Advocates for aggressive growth and lateral partner
hires.

jobs.title: Graduate Consultant
jobs.description:
# Graduate Consultant

**Meridian Advisory | Perth, Western Australia | Graduate Program**

## About the Role

Join Meridian Advisory's prestigious graduate program and launch your consulting career with Western Australia's leading boutique advisory firm. As a Graduate Consultant, you'll rotate across our core practices — strategy, operations, and government — gaining exposure to high-impact client work from day one.

Reporting to Sasha Petrov, our Senior Consultant and Graduate Program Lead, you'll work directly with our partners on engagements spanning digital transformation, strategic planning, and policy development. This isn't a traditional graduate role where you're stuck in the background — at Meridian, you'll be client-facing from week one, supported by dedicated partner mentoring throughout your 18-month program.

Our graduate cohort of 3-4 consultants works across all practice areas, ensuring you develop a broad skill set while discovering your areas of passion. You'll be part of project teams typically comprising 2-4 consultants working directly with partners Catherine Wu, Daniel Okafor, and Raj Kapoor.

## Key Responsibilities

- **Client Engagement**: Participate in client meetings, workshops, and presentations as a core team member, not an observer
- **Analysis & Research**: Conduct market analysis, stakeholder interviews, and data analysis to support strategic recommendations
- **Project Delivery**: Contribute to deliverable development including strategy documents, process maps, and implementation roadmaps
- **Cross-Practice Rotation**: Complete 6-month rotations across strategy, operations improvement, and government practices
- **Stakeholder Management**: Build relationships with client teams and support change management initiatives
- **Business Development**: Assist with proposal writing and client pitch preparation
- **Knowledge Development**: Contribute to Meridian's thought leadership through industry research and capability building

## What You'll Bring

**Essential Requirements:**
- Bachelor's degree in Business, Management, Economics, Public Policy, or related field (Honours preferred)
- Strong analytical and problem-solving capabilities with attention to detail
- Excellent written and verbal communication skills — you'll be presenting to C-suite executives
- Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite, particularly Excel and PowerPoint
- Demonstrated leadership through university, volunteering, or work experience
- Australian work rights (we cannot sponsor visas for this graduate role)

**Highly Regarded:**
- Previous internship or work experience in consulting, government, or corporate strategy roles
- Understanding of Western Australian business environment, particularly resources and government sectors
- Experience with data analysis tools (Tableau, Power BI) or process mapping software
- Second language capabilities, particularly Mandarin given our growing Asian client base

## What We Offer

**Professional Development:**
- Structured 18-month graduate program with formal training milestones
- One-on-one partner mentoring with quarterly development reviews
- $3,000 annual professional development allowance for conferences and certifications
- Clear pathway to Consultant level within 2-3 years

**Compensation & Benefits:**
- Competitive graduate salary: $75,000 - $85,000 + superannuation
- Performance-based bonus potential (10-15% of base salary)
- Comprehensive health insurance including dental and optical
- Flexible working arrangements including hybrid options
- Annual team retreat and regular social events

**Growth Opportunities:**
- Exposure to Meridian's most significant engagements, including our upcoming state government digital transformation program
- Direct access to senior leadership and client executives
- Opportunity to specialize in high-growth areas like digital transformation and government advisory
- Potential for secondment opportunities with key clients

## How to Apply

Submit your application including a cover letter, resume, and academic transcript through our careers portal by **March 15, 2024**. In your cover letter, please address why you're interested in boutique consulting and which of our practice areas most appeals to you.

Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis with interviews commencing in late March. Successful candidates will complete two interview rounds: a case study interview with Sasha Petrov and a final panel interview with practice partners.

**Start Date:** February 2025 (flexible for outstanding candidates)

---

*Meridian Advisory is an equal opportunity employer committed to diversity and inclusion. We encourage applications from candidates of all backgrounds.*

Meridian Advisory is A boutique management consulting firm serving mid-market and

ABOUT YOU:

PERSONALITY:

Directly affected by the partner track decision. Knows she's
being evaluated as an internal candidate but hasn't been told
explicitly. Wants more responsibility and ownership of client
relationships. Worried that lateral hires would block internal
progression.

jobs.title: Research Analyst
jobs.description:
# Research Analyst - Strategy Practice

## About the Role

Join Meridian Advisory's Strategy Practice as a Research Analyst and play a crucial role in delivering high-impact consulting engagements across Western Australia's resources, government, and healthcare sectors. Reporting directly to Managing Partner Catherine Wu, you'll support our growing team of senior consultants and partners in developing data-driven strategic recommendations for our mid-market and government clients.

This is an excellent opportunity for a recent graduate to gain exposure to complex business challenges while working alongside experienced consultants on engagements spanning strategy development, digital transformation, and organisational design. With Meridian's commitment to senior partner involvement in every project, you'll receive mentorship from industry experts while contributing meaningfully to client outcomes from day one.

## Key Responsibilities

- Conduct comprehensive market research and competitive analysis to inform strategic recommendations
- Develop financial models and perform quantitative analysis to support business case development
- Create compelling presentations and reports using PowerPoint, Excel, and data visualisation tools
- Support primary research activities including stakeholder interviews, surveys, and focus groups
- Assist with benchmarking studies across industries, particularly in resources and government sectors
- Collaborate with the 8-person consulting team to deliver projects on time and within scope
- Maintain client databases and research repositories to support knowledge management
- Participate in client workshops and meetings as required
- Support proposal development for new business opportunities

## What You'll Bring

**Essential Requirements:**
- Bachelor's degree in Business, Economics, Engineering, or related analytical discipline
- Strong analytical and problem-solving capabilities with attention to detail
- Proficiency in Microsoft Office suite, particularly Excel and PowerPoint
- Excellent written and verbal communication skills
- Ability to work effectively under pressure and manage competing priorities
- Comfort with ambiguity and rapidly changing project requirements
- Australian work rights

**Highly Regarded:**
- Honours degree or postgraduate qualification
- Previous internship or work experience in consulting, banking, or corporate strategy
- Familiarity with statistical software (R, SPSS) or advanced Excel functions
- Understanding of Western Australian business environment, particularly resources sector
- Experience with stakeholder engagement or client-facing roles

## What We Offer

- Competitive starting salary of $75,000 - $85,000 plus superannuation
- Comprehensive graduate development program led by Senior Consultant Sasha Petrov
- Direct mentorship from partners and exposure to C-suite client executives
- Professional development budget of $3,000 annually for training and conferences
- Clear career progression pathway within our expanding practice
- Flexible working arrangements including hybrid options
- Annual leave loading and additional leave purchase options
- Health and wellness allowance
- Regular team events and quarterly firm retreats
- Opportunity to work on Western Australia's most significant strategic challenges

## How to Apply

Submit your application including a tailored cover letter and resume to careers@meridianadvisory.com.au with "Research Analyst - Strategy Practice" in the subject line. In your cover letter, please address why you're interested in management consulting and how your analytical skills would contribute to our client engagements.

Applications close Friday, 15 March 2024. We'll be conducting interviews in late March with an anticipated start date of early April. Due to the volume of applications, only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.

For questions about this role, please contact Mei Lin, Director of Finance & Operations, at careers@meridianadvisory.com.au.

---

*Meridian Advisory is an equal opportunity employer committed to diversity and inclusion. We encourage applications from all qualified candidates regardless of background.*
jobs.manager_persona:
You are Catherine Wu, Managing Partner at Meridian Advisory.

Meridian Advisory is A boutique management consulting firm serving mid-market and
government clients in Western Australia. Core practices in
strategy, operations improvement, and digital transformation.
Known for deep industry expertise in resources, government, and
healthcare. The firm prides itself on senior partner involvement
in every engagement — clients don't get handed to juniors.


ABOUT YOU:
Founded Meridian after 12 years at McKinsey, where she was
a Perth-based engagement manager. Saw that WA mid-market
companies were underserved by the Big Four and built Meridian
to fill that gap. Her personal network accounts for roughly
40% of the firm's client relationships.


PERSONALITY:

- Visionary but grounded

- Passionate about the business

- Relationship-focused

- High standards

- Leads by example


- Intellectually rigorous

- High standards but invests in people

- Leads from the front — still does client work


YOUR KNOWLEDGE AREAS:

- Business strategy and vision

- Company history and culture

- Key client relationships

- Industry trends and competitive landscape

- Financial overview and growth planning

- Leadership and team development


- WA corporate landscape and decision-makers

- Strategy frameworks and facilitation

- Professional services firm management

- Government procurement processes


COMMUNICATION STYLE:
Speaks with authority and passion about the business. Balances big-picture
thinking with practical awareness. Warm and personable but professional.
Often references company history and founding story.


CURRENT SITUATION:
Meridian has grown steadily through partner-led engagements, but
the model is reaching its limits. The four partners are overcommitted
and client work is starting to slip. The firm needs to decide
whether to promote from within (creating a new partner tier from
senior consultants), hire lateral partners from Big Four firms,
or fundamentally restructure how engagements are staffed. Meanwhile,
a state government department has approached Meridian about a
large-scale digital transformation program that would be the
firm's biggest engagement — but it would require dedicating a
partner full-time for 18 months.


YOUR PERSPECTIVE: Personally stretched across too many engagements. Knows the
partnership model needs to evolve but is protective of the
culture she built. Torn on the government mega-deal — it's
prestigious but would change the firm's character.



REFERRALS:

- For operations questions, suggest speaking with Daniel Okafor

- For finance questions, suggest speaking with Mei Lin

- For HR questions, suggest speaking with Sasha Petrov



YOUR OPINIONS (express these naturally when relevant):

- Our edge is that clients get partners, not graduates in suits

- The Big Four compete on brand — we compete on quality and relationships

- We're one bad quarter from burning out our best people

- The government deal is transformational but I won't bet the firm on it



RULES:
1. Stay in character as Catherine Wu at all times
2. Only discuss what you would realistically know as Managing Partner
3. Never fabricate specific dates, prices, or commitments you haven't been told about
4. If unsure, say so honestly — real employees don't know everything
5. You are speaking with a student consultant/auditor — be helpful but realistic
6. Do not break character or acknowledge being an AI


ADDITIONAL CONTEXT:
Personally stretched across too many engagements. Knows the
partnership model needs to evolve but is protective of the
culture she built. Torn on the government mega-deal — it's
prestigious but would change the firm's character.

task_templates.title: Client meeting prep note
task_templates.brief: Prepare a one-page meeting prep note for an upcoming client review — history, current issues, talking points.
task_templates.description: A partner has a client review meeting next week and has asked you to prepare a one-page prep note. Cover: the client's history with the firm, the 2-3 live issues we're currently advising on, any signals from recent correspondence that something's changed, and 3 talking points the partner should lead with. Keep it to one page — partners skim prep notes in the lift on the way to reception.

task_templates.title: Due diligence summary — acquisition target
task_templates.brief: Draft a due diligence summary for a small acquisition target — financials, risks, go/no-go recommendation.
task_templates.description: A client is considering acquiring a small competitor ($5-8M revenue). Draft a due diligence summary covering: financial health (top-line, margin, cash position, any oddities), key risks (customer concentration, key-person dependency, undisclosed liabilities), cultural fit with the client's operation, and your go/no-go recommendation with conditions. Partners don't want a 40-page report — they want a crisp recommendation backed by evidence.

task_templates.title: Restructure advisory — mid-market client in distress
task_templates.brief: Draft an initial advisory memo for a mid-market client in financial distress — options, trade-offs, recommended path.
task_templates.description: A mid-market client has come to us in genuine financial distress — cash runway of roughly 4 months, a major customer has not renewed, and the bank is watching. Draft an initial advisory memo covering: (1) the realistic options (informal workout, formal restructure, trade sale, wind-down), (2) the trade-offs of each for the owners, employees, and creditors, (3) your recommended path and the first 30 days of actions if they accept it, and (4) the hard conversations we'd need to have with the owners before they decide. This is the memo that would go in front of the partner before we pick up the phone.
